tldr: Headlines across all eight outlet groups uniformly foreground the human cost of mutual strikes — missile barrages on Kyiv, Sumy, and Odesa; Ukrainian drones hitting Russia; single-day death counts of 2–13 — while a secondary thread from Foreign Policy and Kyiv Post surfaces Russia's Black Sea targeting and Ukraine's shadow-fleet campaign as a distinct economic-warfare narrative invisible in every other outlet's headline.

evidence.quote: 10 injured in overnight Russian missile and drone strikes
Ukraine: Six dead as Russia targets Sumy and Odesa
At least 4 killed, 7 wounded when Ukraine drone strikes hit Russia
2 Killed, 6 Wounded After Russian Ballistic Missiles Strike Kyiv
Russian forces launch 853 strikes on Zaporizhzhia region
Russia strikes Kyiv for the second time in a week
At Least 3 Killed in Ukrainian Attacks Across Russia
Russia launches deadly barrage on Kyiv region
Russian and Ukrainian attacks kill at least 13

frames.sub_frame: Black Sea ports, shadow fleet, sanctions war

evidence.quote: Russia Shifts Its Focus to the Black Sea
Ukraine Says It Hit 159 Russian Shadow Fleet Vessels in 12 Days

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only headline to frame the war through a strategic-economic lens — Russia targeting Black Sea trade routes — vs every other outlet's casualty-count register.
Only outlet to headline Ukraine's offensive against the Russian shadow fleet as a named 12-day campaign, making sanctions-evasion logistics the advertised story rather than civilian strikes.
Only headline to embed Russia's Kyiv barrage in the NATO summit context ('on eve of NATO summit'), adding a diplomatic-timing signal invisible in all other outlets' casualty-focused headlines.
Only outlet to describe strikes on Kyiv and Odessa as targeting 'drone industry' and port infrastructure per MOD language, framing the attacks as military-industrial rather than residential/civilian.

bottom_line: At the headline layer, the Russia-Ukraine war story is almost entirely a bilateral casualty ledger — how many killed, in which city, by which weapon — with Foreign Policy and Kyiv Post alone widening the register to an economic war over Black Sea shipping lanes and Russian sanctions-evading fleet operations.
